issues.problem: Sample query uses fabricated table name `DWH_dbo.SomeHistoryCostsFact` instead of a real downstream consumer. Analysts copying this query will get an error.
No date range mentioned. Even for a static dictionary, should explicitly note 'no temporal dimension' rather than silently omitting.
Operation type ID-range categorizations (0-11 orders, 12-19 positions, etc.) are inferred from names, not sourced from documentation. Should carry an inference caveat.
No explicit Phase Gate Checklist section. Footer says 'Phases: 7/14' without specifying which phases were completed vs. skipped.
Tier 1 definition includes parenthetical '(no upstream wiki available for this object)' — either omit the unused tier or keep the legend clean.

verdict.regeneration_feedback: Minor fixes only (wiki passes): (1) Replace `DWH_dbo.SomeHistoryCostsFact` in Section 7.3 with a real downstream table or remove the query with a note that downstream consumers were not identified. (2) Add explicit note in Section 1 that the table has no temporal dimension. (3) Add '(inferred from operation type names)' caveat to ID-range categorizations in Sections 1 and 2.1. (4) Add an explicit Phase Gate Checklist section.

skipped_phases: P2/P3 status unclear — footer says 7/14 phases but no checklist provided
